items.title: Heavy rain triggers flash floods in Bintulu, vehicles stranded as water spills into homes
items.summary: BINTULU, June 11 — Several roads and residential areas here have been inundated following heavy rain since early Thursday morning. The downpour caused flash floods in a number of low-lying areas, with floodwaters rising rapidly and affecting traffic flow on several roads across the town. Among the affected areas were residential neighbourhoods where overflowing drains led to water spilling onto roads and into house compounds.

items.title: Op Tiris 4.0: Over 68,000 litres of subsidised petrol seized nationwide
items.summary: © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Sarawak JKR hands over RM100m schools in Bintulu to MOE, boosting education demand
items.summary: BINTULU, June 11 — The Sarawak Public Works Department (JKR) has handed over two new school projects here worth more than RM100 million to the Ministry of Education (MOE) to meet the growing demand for educational facilities in rapidly developing areas in Bintulu. In a statement, Sarawak JKR said the projects handed over were Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Samalaju, valued at RM57.62 million and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Orang Kaya Mohammad Bintulu, worth RM42.74 million. Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Samalaju.

items.title: Anwar hails Malaysia–Japan ties as vital amid global uncertainty, energy security and regional stability
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — The close relationship between Malaysia and Japan is becoming increasingly important amid a more challenging and uncertain global environment, with both countries sharing common interests in strengthening energy security, economic resilience, technological development and regional stability, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. In a Facebook post yesterday, he said his first official visit to Japan, which began on Monday and lasted three days, marked an important milestone in further strengthening the special relationship between the two countries, since diplomatic ties were established in 1957. Anwar said Malaysia and Japan had since continued to grow as close friends and trusted strategic partners.

items.title: MCMC says reliable telecoms vital for RTS Link success, highlights collaboration during Johor site visit
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 11 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has committed to fully supporting the upcoming Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link by providing reliable digital connectivity infrastructure to ensure smooth operations of the cross-border rail network, according to a post on the RTS Link JB-SG official Facebook page. He highlighted this during a working visit by an MCMC delegation to the RTS Link project site at the Bukit Chagar Station and Bangunan Sultan Ibrahim yesterday to gain firsthand insight into the project’s progress and ongoing developments. The MCMC delegation was welcomed by the RTS Link’s Electrical, Mechanical and Building Services (EMBS) Project Director Ir Ardy Suhaidy, and Health, Environment, Safety and Security Chief Syahmarul Zulaihan Marlap.

items.title: ‘Why should I campaign for you again?’: At first meeting since MP resignation, Setiawangsa residents look to Nik Nazmi for answers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — In Setiawangsa last night, a resident stood up at a dialogue session to complain about abandoned cars in Seksyen 6, Wangsa Maju. Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad is no longer the MP for Setiawangsa. One resident, Razif from Seksyen 6, sought Nik Nazmi’s help over abandoned vehicles that he said had become an eyesore.

items.title: Selangor MB says enforcement on illegal Rohingya flats in Hulu Langat dates back to 2016
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 11 — Various actions have been taken by government agencies since 2016 to address issues involving a residential building occupied by Rohingya residents in Kampung Baru Sungai Makau, Mukim Ulu Langat, which recently went viral on social media. Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the measures included the issuance of warning notices, compounds, integrated operations, investigations and enforcement action by various government agencies. “Various measures have been taken since 2016, including the issuance of warning notices, compounds, integrated operations, investigations and enforcement action by the Hulu Langat District and Land Office (PDT), Kajang Municipal Council (MPKj), Selangor Water Management Authority (LUAS), Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Immigration Department, as well as demolition operations,” he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: University student killed in Rawang lorry crash, friend injured
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A university student was killed after the car he was driving collided with a lorry at the Taman Sari traffic light intersection in Rawang, heading towards Country Homes, yesterday. Gombak district police chief Assistant Commissioner Ibrahim Husin said the 2.25pm crash also left a passenger injured. He said initial investigations indicate the car skidded into the opposite lane and collided with an oncoming lorry.

items.title: Air India crash report delayed due to unfinished engine examination, Bloomberg News reports
items.summary: India's Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau will likely issue a status report this week focusing on the reasons behind the delay, with a final report expected within three months, the report added. Reuters first reported last month that Indian officials investigating the crash were preparing an interim report rather than a final one ahead of the first anniversary, as the investigation was deemed complex and time-consuming. Under international rules, a final report is due within a year of an accident.

items.title: Prime minister stresses Unity Government stability, rejects talk of betrayal as PAS ends Bersatu ties
items.summary: TOKYO, June 11 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reaffirmed the Unity Government’s commitment to its coalition partners, saying it would not take any action that could be perceived as a betrayal of the existing political consensus. Anwar, who is Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, said the government remained stable, with all parties continuing to respect the understanding reached to ensure the country’s political stability. Anwar, who is also PKR president, was commenting on claims that several former Bersatu state assemblymen could join PKR following recent developments in the political relationship between Bersatu and PAS.

items.title: #NSTviral: Immigration raid at Johor hypermarket sparks calls for statewide 'clean-up'
items.summary: SENAI: Netizens are calling for a "clean-up" of undocumented foreigners living and working in the Kulai-Senai area after a major Immigration raid at a hypermarket. In a viral video, dozens of detainees were seen chained and ferried away in Immigration trucks. Echoing this sentiment, Aqila Yasmin told authorities to conduct such operations more regularly.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei slumps as chip selloff, geopolitical tensions dent risk appetite
items.summary: The Nikkei was down 1.30 per cent at 63,360.96 as of 0046 GMT. The broader Topix slid 1.60 per cent to 3,787.08. Tech investment conglomerate SoftBank Group dragged the Nikkei index lower, falling 3.60 per cent.

items.title: Umno vows to back Unity Government until GE16, citing Sultan Ibrahim’s decree on stability, says sec-gen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Umno will continue to support the Unity Government that was formed after the 15th General Election (GE15) until the next general election, in line with the decree of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. Umno Secretary-General Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said this stance was decided during the Umno Supreme Council (MKT) meeting chaired by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, who is also the Umno President, at Menara Dato’ Onn here yesterday. At the same time, he stated that the agreement to form the Unity Government at the Federal level does not bind any state government.

items.title: Microsoft's Xbox plans for major layoffs next month
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Microsoft's Xbox division is planning major layoffs next month and significant cuts to marketing and other budgets, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday. The job cuts would mark the first major restructuring under Asha Sharma, who took charge as chief executive officer of the gaming unit in February. Xbox has faced mounting challenges in recent years as Microsoft's bet on subscriptions and cloud gaming failed to offset declining console sales and a shortage of blockbuster titles.

items.title: Nine‑year‑old dies after SUV hit him walking roadside near housing area in Rembau
items.summary: REMBAU, June 11 — A nine-year-old boy was killed after being struck by a sport utility vehicle (SUV) while walking along KM1 of Jalan Program Perumahan Rakyat Termiskin (PPRT) Legong Ulu in Kota here yesterday. Acting Rembau police chief DSP Azmi Ali said that in the incident, which occurred at about 5.30pm, the victim was pronounced dead at the scene after sustaining severe head injuries. “The body was taken to the Forensic Unit of Rembau Hospital for a post-mortem examination, while the driver did not sustain any injuries,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Two arrested over knife-point robbery of e-hailing driver
items.summary: District police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said police received a report at 2.46am of a knife-point robbery at the Tun Sri Lanang Bus Terminal. "Acting on information, police arrested a local woman in her 20s near the bus terminal at about 11am. He said police also seized a knife believed to have been used in the incident.

items.title: Wildlife parts worth RM160,000 seized, five Myanmar citizens arrested
items.summary: KUALA KRAI: Five Myanmar citizens have been arrested for allegedly killing a bear and harvesting its paws and gall bladder for the black market. Perhilitan director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said wildlife parts worth an estimated RM160,000 were seized during the operation. "Inspectors found various butchered wildlife carcasses inside the house, including a large barrel containing wildlife meat and a spear believed to have been used for hunting," he said.

items.title: ECB poised for 'insurance hike' as Iran war fans euro zone inflation
items.summary: FRANKFURT: The European Central Bank (ECB) is all but certain to raise interest rates on Thursday in the hope of nipping higher inflation in the bud before a surge in energy costs triggered by the Iran war spreads more broadly across the euro zone economy. The well-telegraphed move would come as inflation in the 21-country currency bloc is already above three per cent, well in excess of the ECB's two per cent target, and economic growth is very weak - a backdrop that has economists split over the case for tighter policy. Thursday's hike would be the first in nearly three years and take the ECB's benchmark deposit rate to 2.25 per cent from two per cent.

items.title: Man held in Kota Belud over aggressive behaviour, property damage
items.summary: KOTA BELUD: Police have arrested a man believed to be suffering from a mental disorder following reports that he had acted aggressively, injured people and damaged property around Kota Belud town. District police chief Superintendent Shahrudin Mat Husain said the arrest followed several police reports and public complaints over the individual's behaviour, which was believed to pose a threat to public safety. He said the man was taken to Kota Belud Hospital before being referred to the Psychiatry Department at Mesra Bukit Padang Hospital in Kota Kinabalu for further treatment.

items.title: Ringgit opens higher against major currencies, eases vs US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against major currencies today but eased marginally against the US dollar after stronger-than-expected United States inflation reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) could keep interest rates higher for longer. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Consumer Price Index (CPI) was the main focus overnight, with inflation accelerating to 4.2 per cent in May, the highest level since May 2023. The local currency traded higher against a basket of major currencies.

items.title: OPEC oil output lowest since at least 2000 as US blockade squeezes Iran
items.summary: LONDON: OPEC oil output in May hit its lowest level in more than two decades, a Reuters survey found, as a US naval blockade cut Iran's exports and Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz slashed exports by other Gulf producers. Output by the 11-member Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) fell by 1.06 million barrels per day (bpd) month-on-month to 16.13 million bpd, the survey found. Iran's exports of crude oil and condensate fell to their lowest level in at least six years.

items.title: Year 3 pupil killed while crossing the road
items.summary: REMBAU: A Year Three pupil was killed after being struck by a sport utility vehicle (SUV) while crossing Jalan PPRT Legong Ulu in Kota here yesterday. Deputy district police chief Deputy Superintendent Azmi Ali said the incident occurred at about 5.30pm. The victim's body was taken to the Rembau Hospital Forensic Unit for a post-mortem, while the driver was unhurt.

items.title: "I love the inflation": Trump comment on latest price jump sparks backlash
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday brushed off concerns about consumer inflation surging to a fresh three-year high in May, on the back of soaring energy prices caused by his war in Iran. "Trump really said, 'I love the inflation.' "Trump said the quiet part out loud – he loves inflation.

items.title: Frasers Group launches US$2.3bil takeover offer for struggling Hugo Boss
items.summary: GERMANY: Frasers Group, the retailer controlled by British billionaire Mike Ashley, launched a €2 billion (US$2.31 billion) takeover offer for German fashion brand Hugo Boss on Wednesday. Frasers, the biggest shareholder in Hugo Boss with a 26.06 per cent stake, is offering €38 per share in cash for the remaining shares — a 4.3 per cent premium to the €36.44 closing price of Hugo Boss stock on Wednesday. "To facilitate further investment by Frasers in Hugo Boss, Frasers has decided to make a voluntary public takeover offer to all Hugo Boss shareholders for all Hugo Boss shares not directly held by Frasers," Frasers said in a statement.

items.title: Gold slides 3pct as Middle East escalation fuels inflation, rate-hike concerns
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices fell more than three per cent on Wednesday as fears of a wider US-backed war with Iran stoked concerns about interest rate hikes to curb rising inflation, with investors focusing on key US data for clues on the monetary policy path. Spot gold was down 3.5 per cent at US$4,111.95 per ounce by 2:26 p.m. EDT (1826 GMT), its lowest level since March 23US gold futures for August delivery settled 3.6 per cent lower at US$4,133.3. Spot silver fell 0.8 per cent to US$64.83 per ounce, platinum dropped 2.6 per cent to US$1,681.88, and palladium rose 0.7 per cent to US$1,230.41.

items.title: Oil settles up nearly US$2 after Trump threatens to hit Iran 'very hard'
items.summary: NEW YORK: Oil prices closed up nearly US$2 on Wednesday after President Donald Trump said the US is going to attack Iran "very hard" if no peace deal is finalised. Brent crude futures settled at US$93.10 a barrel, up US$1.65 or 1.8 per cent. US crude futures finished at US$90.03 a barrel, up US$1.83, or two per cent.

items.title: Iran says will target any vessel traffic in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's military command said Thursday it will target any ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz, after it struck two vessels attempting to pass through the strategic waterway, as talks to end the war faltered. The Khatam al-Anbiya command said Thursday, "any vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz will be targeted", adding that the strait is now "completely closed to all types of vessel", according to the Tasnim news agency. Iran's Revolutionary Guards navy, quoted by state television IRIB and the Mehr agency, said that "two ships attempting to illegally pass through the Strait of Hormuz were hit".

items.title: Wall Street indexes fall more than 1pct, hit by tech, Iran war worries
items.summary: NEW YORK: The major US stock indexes ended more than one per cent lower on Wednesday, with chipmaker shares extending recent declines and renewed tensions between the US and Iran adding to investor uncertainty. President Donald Trump said the US would attack Iran again if no peace deal is secured. Industrials dropped 3.4 per cent, leading sector decliners.

items.title: Investing in recession-proof businesses
items.summary: These businesses continue to generate revenue and profits even when economic conditions deteriorate. A recession-proof business is one that provides products or services that people need regardless of economic conditions. Companies operating in these sectors often experience less severe earnings declines than businesses dependent on discretionary spending.

items.title: Petronas rejigs upstream portfolio as UK's EnQuest takes PSC stakes [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) is transferring operatorship and stakes in several producing upstream assets in Peninsular Malaysia and Sarawak to EnQuest Petroleum Production Malaysia Ltd. The series of agreements signed by its upstream arm Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd is as part of the national oil company's efforts to optimise its portfolio and extend the life of mature oil and gas fields. Petronas said the move aligns with its strategy of unlocking value from producing assets through partnerships, while supporting Malaysia's long-term energy security and industry development.

items.title: Takaful industry eyes cautious outlook after 4.7pct growth in 2025 [WATCH]
items.summary: Malaysian Takaful Association (MTA) interim chairman Borhanudin Samsudin said the industry remained resilient last year, with total gross contributions rising 4.73 per cent to RM16.38 billion. "Collectively, takaful operators paid out RM10.61 billion in benefits in 2025. Of this, 74.6 per cent or RM7.91 billion was disbursed by family takaful, while general takaful honoured RM2.70 billion," he said at MTA's media briefing on the 2025 takaful industry performance.

items.title: Vance says Israeli PM has 'gotten some things wrong'
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US Vice President JD Vance acknowledged differences with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in an interview excerpt released Wednesday, saying the Israeli leader had "gotten some things wrong" in the Middle East war. Vance emphasised that Israel is a close partner of the United States, but noted that "sometimes, we have interests that are perfectly aligned and sometimes, we have interests that are misaligned," according to a clip posted on X by CBS News. "I think that those conversations sometimes are better left in private," Vance said.

items.title: White House defends Cuba sanctions as UN warns of harm
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Trump administration defended its sanctions on Cuba on Wednesday after the United Nations' human rights chief warned US actions were causing "widespread harm to the population and endangering lives". Washington has imposed sanctions on an array of Cuban entities and people, including the island nation's president, as it seeks to intensify pressure on Cuba's communist leaders. US President Donald Trump has repeatedly talked about the US taking action against Cuba.

items.title: Pearly Kee, Penang Nyonya culinary icon, dies
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Renowned Penang-based Nyonya culinary educator, food ambassador and author Pearly Kee, 69, died yesterday after a battle with cancer. Kee was widely recognised for her efforts in preserving and promoting traditional Nyonya cuisine and Penang's rich culinary heritage. Kee leaves behind a lasting legacy as one of Penang's most respected custodians of Nyonya culinary traditions.

items.title: US judge denies request to temporarily halt Trump's abandoned 'weaponisation' fund
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A US judge on Wednesday blocked a request to temporarily halt President Donald Trump's US$1.8 billion "weaponisation" fund, citing Justice Department statements and court filings that said the administration was abandoning the fund. Leon asked Justice Department attorney Andrew Block why department leaders would not formally rescind the fund. Leon also asked Block to reckon with Trump's statements of support for the fund and acting Attorney General Todd Blanche telling lawmakers the fund was not moving forward.

items.title: 'US-China tech rivalry a boon for Malaysia'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The United States-China technology rivalry could create new opportunities for Malaysia to attract supply chain investments as companies seek to diversify beyond geopolitical flashpoints. Universiti Teknologi Mara Business Management Faculty senior lecturer Dr Mohamad Idham Md Razak said the expanding US restrictions presented opportunities and challenges for Malaysia. He said Malaysia could benefit as companies accelerate supply chain diversification amid increased scrutiny of Chinese firms.

items.title: US launches new strikes after Trump threatens to attack Iran 'very hard'
items.summary: WASHINGTON/DUBAI: The United States began a fresh round of strikes against multiple targets overnight in Iran, the US military said on Wednesday, hours after President Donald Trump vowed new attacks if no peace deal is secured. "The strikes are in response to Iran's unwarranted and continued aggression," the military's Central Command said in a post on X, adding the strikes began after midnight in Teheran. Iran's top joint military command responded to the US strikes, warning that it would fire on any vessel attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, which has been largely closed for months.

items.title: Canada moves to ban under-16s from social media, regulate AI
items.summary: MONTREAL: Canada's culture minister on Wednesday introduced legislation that would ban children under 16 from having social media accounts and require AI chatbot services to limit production of harmful content. The legislation would ban social media accounts for children under 16 years old, the statement said, adding that there be an exemption "pathway" for companies if they can demonstrate "sufficient safeguards" for children. Social media services, including adult content platforms, would also face new requirements under the law to "mitigate risks associated with exposure" to various categories of harmful content and apply labels to synthetically generated content.

items.title: Pas-Bersatu split hands advantage to rivals, say analysts [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PAS' decision to end political cooperation with Bersatu could not have come at a worse time for the opposition, with Johor and Negri Sembilan heading for state elections. "As such, Pas and Bersatu face a challenge to defend their seats in both states. In the 2022 Johor election, PN won three of the 56 seats.

items.title: UKM Study finds Malaysian students struggling with basic maths as reliance on calculators and ChatGPT grows
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Malaysia risks raising a generation proficient in artificial intelligence (AI) technology but lacking strong critical thinking and analytical skills if numeracy and reasoning abilities are not strengthened from an early age, according to an expert. Senior lecturer at the Department of Electrical, Electronic and Systems Engineering, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM), Associate Professor Kalaivani Chellappan said the concern stems from the growing use of AI and digital technologies in daily life, particularly in learning, research and completing tasks. The data analytics and digital health expert said that although technology can improve efficiency and facilitate the learning process, over-reliance on it may undermine the mastery of basic skills essential for developing rational thinking and sound judgment.

items.title: Is Malaysia ready for an era of ageing drivers as population grows older?
items.summary: The family is aware that due to age-related fragility, elderly drivers are more vulnerable to injuries and fatalities in road accidents. According to police data, more than 19,800 elderly drivers were involved in road accidents across Malaysia between 2019 and 2024. Malaysia’s current regulatory framework for elderly drivers is not adequately prepared for an ageing population, said Prof Dr Muhammad Zaly Shah, president of the Chartered Institute of Logistics and Transport Malaysia (CILTM).

items.title: JPJ to haul extreme bike modifiers to court under tougher enforcement push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Following a rising trend in extreme motorcycle modifications, the Road Transport Department (JPJ) is adopting a new enforcement approach by taking these cases straight to court under Section 42 of the Road Transport Act 1987. JPJ senior enforcement director Datuk Muhammad Kifli Ma Hassan said so far three investigation papers have been opened under the provision and received consent for prosecution from the Deputy Public Prosecutor. He said the move marks a new strategy for JPJ, which previously focused on standard enforcement actions such as issuing compounds and seizing vehicles.

items.title: Fire Dept reviewing SOPs for mountain climbing after missing hiker cases
items.summary: MARANG, June 10 — The Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) is assessing the need to enhance standard operating procedures (SOPs) and regulations governing extreme sports, particularly mountain climbing activities. Its director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said the move is aimed at preventing incidents involving missing hikers, including the latest case involving Jaslinda Saludin, who went missing on Gunung Batu Putih in Ipoh for 14 days before being found safe in the Lubuk Gaharu Orang Asli settlement. He said the department would hold discussions with relevant parties, including the Forestry Department and state park management authorities, which have jurisdiction over locations frequented by hikers.

items.title: Sabah Wildlife Dept seizes 300 turtle eggs in Tuaran, arrest man
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 10 – A joint special operation conducted by the Wildlife Crime Bureau (WCB) and the Sabah Wildlife Department has led to the arrest of a 37-year-old Malaysian man in Kampung Serusup, Tuaran, about 40km from here for alleged possession of what is believed to be protected turtle eggs. The operation seized approximately 300 eggs believed to be from green turtles, a protected species in Sabah. The Sabah Wildlife Department emphasised that it takes any form of illegal possession, trade, or smuggling of wildlife very seriously.

items.title: Cops launch manhunt for suspect after fatal stabbing in Ipoh
items.summary: Perak Tengah district police chief Supt Hafezul Helmi Hamzah said police received information about the incident at about 5.49am. He said further investigations revealed that the suspect fled the scene in a black Proton Wira bearing registration number WGX 3707. Information can also be channelled to any nearby police station,” he said.

items.title: Gold bullion forfeiture challenge linked to ex-army chief’s wife set for July 31
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Sessions Court here today allowed the government’s application to issue a third-party notice in the Gazette against anyone wishing to challenge the forfeiture of 3.85 kg of gold bullion seized from an acquaintance of Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin, the wife of former Army Chief Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan. Judge Suzana Hussin set July 31 as the hearing date for any party with an interest in the gold bullion, which was seized from contractor Mohamad Amirul Yusrie Aminorddin @ Ab Kadir, 31. Also seized were jewellery worth RM294,039, two diamond rings valued at RM20,000, 3.7 kg of gold bullion worth RM2.5 million, a 150-g gold medallion worth RM102,000, and two gold dinars weighing 8.5 g valued at RM5,780.

items.title: Malaysia records 17.5 million tourist arrivals by May as VM2026 campaign gains momentum
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 10 — Foreign visitor arrivals under the Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026) campaign are on a strong trajectory, with 17.5 million tourists recorded as of May, says Deputy Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Chiew Choon Man. He said that in pursuit of the campaign’s target of 47 million visitors, the ministry, through Tourism Malaysia, had organised various promotional programmes both domestically and abroad to attract more tourists to Malaysia. Meanwhile, Chiew today launched the special registration number series “V 1 MY” to “V 9999 MY” in conjunction with VM2026.

items.title: PTPTN allocates RM60.1m in advance loans to help 40,099 new IPTA, polytechnic students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated RM60.1 million in Loan Advance Payment (WPP) assistance to 40,099 new diploma students enrolling at public higher education institutions (IPTAs) and polytechnics for the June 2026 intake. PTPTN said each eligible student would receive RM1,500 through the WPP to help cover part of the initial expenses associated with enrolling in higher education institutions, thereby easing the financial burden on their families. It added that the WPP is offered to Malaysians who agree to receive the facility when applying for admission to public higher education institutions through UPUOnline or the respective public university admission portals.

items.title: Sabah police bust 12 scam call centres, arrest 341 suspects
items.summary: Sabah Police Commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikun said the special operation was carried out by the State Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) following continuous intelligence gathering and monitoring. He said the raids were conducted in Kota Kinabalu, Labuan, Tawau, Sandakan and Penampang. Jauteh said investigations revealed that the suspects were assigned various roles within the syndicates, including operations supervisors, customer service officers, system programmers, project managers, financial administrators, and personnel directly contacting victims.

items.title: High Court in KL upholds RM60,000 award against man who shared ex-girlfriend’s intimate photos online
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 10 — The Kuala Lumpur High Court today upheld a Tribunal for Anti-Sexual Harassment (TAGS) award in favour of a woman whose ex-boyfriend shared her intimate photos online without her consent. “This decision by the Kuala Lumpur High Court reinforces TAGS’ role as a fast, simple and effective justice mechanism in providing remedies to victims of sexual harassment, including digital sexual harassment,” the statement read. KPWKM said TAGS, established on March 8, 2024, provides an alternative channel to civil courts for hearing and deciding sexual harassment complaints.

items.title: AirBorneo apologises for Sabah, Sarawak flight disruptions, commits to service recovery
items.summary: KUCHING, June 10 — AirBorneo has apologised to guests affected by recent flight delays and cancellations across Sarawak and Sabah, acknowledging the disruption, inconvenience and uncertainty experienced by travellers and their families over the past few days. AirBorneo chief executive officer Megat Ardian Wira Mohd Aminuddin said the airline understands the frustration, inconvenience and uncertainty this has caused. Megat Ardian Wira said AirBorneo has implemented additional operational and maintenance measures to strengthen fleet resilience and support the progressive restoration of normal services.

items.title: Anwar: Japan visit strengthens investment, AI and trade ties
items.summary: He said Malaysia highlighted several key priorities in the meeting with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, including high-value investments, new technologies and cooperation in the field of artificial intelligence (AI). “We prioritise this because we need cooperation and support not only from the Japanese government but also from major Japanese companies,” he said at the final press conference of the three-day official visit that began on Monday. The visit was also used to hold meetings with major Japanese companies, including Mitsubishi Corporation, ENEOS Corporation, DENSO Corporation, and Marubeni Corporation, to explore opportunities for expanding cooperation and investment in Malaysia.

items.title: Perlis govt carries on as usual amid Perikatan leadership uncertainty, says Shahidan Kassim
items.summary: KANGAR, June 10 — The Perlis state government will continue to function as usual pending a decision by the Perikatan Nasional (PN) leadership following PAS’ move to end its political cooperation with Bersatu. Perlis PN chairman and PAS Perlis deputy commissioner III, Datuk Seri Dr Shahidan Kassim, said efforts to safeguard the welfare of the people would remain the coalition’s priority despite the political uncertainty. The Perlis state assembly currently comprises six PAS assemblymen, five from Bersatu, and one Pakatan Harapan assemblyman.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain expected across nine states and Labuan, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit nine states and Labuan until 7pm tonight, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In an alert issued at 5pm, MetMalaysia said two northern states are expected to experience these weather conditions: in Kedah, the affected areas are Kuala Muda, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu; while in Penang, they cover Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan. In Perak, the forecast covers the coastal areas of Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Manjung, and Hilir Perak, as well as inland areas of Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim.

items.title: Govt’s RON95, diesel subsidy bill estimated to hit RM3.5b a month, says Finance Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The government’s subsidy bill for RON95 petrol and diesel is projected to remain high at about RM3.5 billion a month at current market prices, amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said. Of the total, subsidies for RON95 petrol are estimated at RM2 billion a month, while diesel subsidies are projected at around RM1.5 billion a month, the ministry said. At the same time, the MADANI government continues to strengthen long-term energy security measures and targeted subsidy initiatives as the global supply crisis persists, the ministry said.

items.title: Bookies beware: Bukit Aman launches Op Soga XI ahead of Fifa World Cup 2026 betting surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has launched Op Soga XI nationwide to intensify enforcement against illegal gambling activities, particularly sports betting and online gambling expected to rise in conjunction with the Fifa World Cup 2026. The operation targets both online and physical gambling activities, including syndicate-run betting platforms, websites and social media accounts promoting illegal wagering. It also covers physical gambling activities such as illegal betting premises, bookmakers, agents, and individuals involved in organising or promoting gambling activities.

items.title: RTM mobilises resources for World Cup coverage as RTM Klik hits seven million downloads
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) is committed to delivering comprehensive coverage of all 104 matches of the FIFA World Cup 2026 across its television and digital platforms. Director-General of Broadcasting Ashwad Ismail said football fans nationwide will be able to follow the tournament through RTM Klik and RTM channels on MYTV and Unifi TV. He said RTM is mobilising its full resources to ensure the quadrennial tournament is accessible to all Malaysians, including those in rural areas.

items.title: Zahid: Malaysian envoys play key role in strengthening bilateral ties abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Malaysian heads of mission representing the country abroad shoulder a significant responsibility in strengthening bilateral ties and upholding the nation’s image on the international stage, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. In a Facebook post today, Ahmad Zahid said he was confident that the experience and expertise of each diplomat would enable them to carry out their duties effectively. Ahmad Zahid made the remarks after receiving a courtesy call from 13 newly appointed Malaysian heads of mission before they departed to assume their postings abroad.

items.title: CAAM monitoring AirBorneo disruptions amid delays and cancellations in Sabah, Sarawak
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 10 — The Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) is closely monitoring recent flight delays, cancellations and schedule changes affecting AirBorneo services across Sabah and Sarawak. CAAM said the airline had attributed the disruptions to operational and maintenance-related issues. While stressing that safety must remain the highest priority in flight operations, the authority reminded AirBorneo that affected consumers must continue to be treated fairly and in accordance with the Malaysian Aviation Consumer Protection Code 2016 (MACPC).

items.title: Non-Muslim religious council objects to Selangor worship-house guidelines, says 5,000-resident rule and commercial-lot restrictions unrealistic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Malaysian Consultative Council of Buddhism, Christianity, Hinduism, Sikhism and Taoism (MCCBCHST) has objected to parts of Selangor’s 2025 planning guidelines for non-Muslim houses of worship, saying several conditions for obtaining prayer spaces were too restrictive. MCCBCHST also objected to restrictions against placing non-Muslim houses of worship in commercial business zones, converting existing buildings into places of worship, and using residential houses for worship purposes. 34/2025 dated November 12, 2025,” the council said.
